title: viola langsdorffii var. ursina
intro: Viola langsdorffii var. ursina, also known as the Langsdorff's alpine violet, is a rare and endangered species of flowering plant native to the mountainous regions of Russia and China. The plant has been used in traditional medicine to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and skin problems, due to its analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Viola langsdorffii var. ursina include reducing pain and inflammation, promoting wound healing, and acting as an antioxidant. Herbal preparations of the plant include infusions, teas, and tinctures, which can be used to extract its medicinal properties and provide relief from various health conditions.
